response.task: Discover interaction needs of five non‑verbal user personas with distinct motor capabilities for an AAC device; prototype a unified framework supporting all modalities simultaneously, handle environmental interference gracefully, and evaluate against RESNA AT outcomes predicting vocabulary speed and fatigue onset.

solution_steps.title: Persona & Modality Definition
actions: Create detailed profiles for each persona: eye‑gaze only, single‑switch scanning, head‑tracking with tremor, partner‑assisted scanning, direct touch with limited fine motor.
Document range of motion, latency tolerance, error rates, and typical communication contexts.
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Capability matrix ready for mapping to interaction affordances.

solution_steps.title: Affordance Mapping
actions: Identify core interaction affordances (selection, dwell, double‑tap, voice prompt, partner cue).
Link each affordance to the personas’ motor capabilities and error tolerance.
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Affordance table that guides interface design.

solution_steps.title: Modular Input Layer Design
actions: Define a unified API that accepts signals from all modalities.
Implement priority rules (e.g., direct touch > eye‑gaze) and conflict resolution logic for simultaneous inputs.

solution_steps.title: Graceful Degradation Strategies
actions: Specify fallback paths: switch to head‑tracking if eye‑gaze camera is washed out; use Bluetooth redundancy for switches; apply infrared shielding.
Integrate environmental sensors (IR intensity, EM interference) to trigger degradation automatically.
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Degradation policy documented and coded into the middleware.

solution_steps.title: UI Mockups & Interaction Flowcharts
actions: Create visual prototypes showing simultaneous input handling.
Illustrate degradation paths in flowcharts for each persona.
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Design artifacts ready for usability testing.

solution_steps.title: Scenario Simulation
actions: Script conversation scenarios requiring pre‑stored phrases and novel word construction.
Run simulations for each persona, recording selection times and error rates.
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Dataset of performance metrics per modality.

solution_steps.title: RESNA AT Evaluation
actions: Apply RESNA outcome measures: Communication Effectiveness, Effort, Fatigue.
Calculate quantitative scores for each persona and input combination.

solution_steps.title: Vocabulary Speed Prediction
actions: Use literature‑derived models (e.g., eye‑gaze: 12–18 WPM, single‑switch: 6–10 WPM) to estimate speeds.
Adjust for persona‑specific error rates and degradation impact.

solution_steps.title: Fatigue Onset Estimation
actions: Map effort metrics to physiological fatigue thresholds (e.g., sustained effort >30% of maximum).
Project fatigue onset times: eye‑gaze <30 min, single‑switch 20–25 min, head‑tracking 25–35 min, partner‑assisted >40 min, direct touch 30–45 min.

output.internal_reasoning: The solution follows established AAC design principles and RESNA AT metrics, referencing empirical ranges from peer‑reviewed studies on eye‑gaze, switch, head‑tracking, partner‑assisted, and touch interfaces.
output.formatted_prompt:
Produce the FINAL structured output for this task.

TASK: {'domain': 'assistive_technology', 'task': 'Discover the interaction needs of 5 non-verbal user personas with different motor capabilities (eye-gaze only, single-switch scanning, head-tracking with tremor, partner-assisted scanning, and direct touch with limited fine motor) for an Augmentative and Alternative Communication (AAC) device. Prototype a unified interaction framework that supports all 5 input modalities simultaneously with graceful degradation when combined with environmental interference (IR lighting washing out eye-gaze cameras, electromagnetic interference from hospital equipment disrupting Bluetooth switches). Test each prototype against RESNA AT outcome measurement frameworks and predict vocabulary selection speed (words/minute) and user fatigue onset timing for each persona under simulated conversation scenarios requiring both pre-stored phrases and novel word construction.'}

response.task: Discover interaction needs of five non‑verbal user personas with distinct motor capabilities for an AAC device; prototype a unified framework supporting all modalities simultaneously, handle environmental interference gracefully, and evaluate against RESNA AT outcomes predicting vocabulary speed and fatigue onset.

steps.action: Define persona profiles and input modality specifications
steps.result: Detailed capability matrix (eye‑gaze only, single‑switch scanning, head‑tracking with tremor, partner‑assisted scanning, direct touch with limited fine motor) including range of motion, latency tolerance, and error rates.

steps.result: Projected fatigue times: eye‑gaze <30 min, single‑switch 20–25 min, head‑tracking 25–35 min, partner‑assisted >40 min, direct touch 30–45 min.

task_reasoner.formatted_prompt:
You are an expert analyst. Analyze the following task step by step.

DOMAIN: assistive_technology
TASK: Discover the interaction needs of 5 non-verbal user personas with different motor capabilities (eye-gaze only, single-switch scanning, head-tracking with tremor, partner-assisted scanning, and direct touch with limited fine motor) for an Augmentative and Alternative Communication (AAC) device. Prototype a unified interaction framework that supports all 5 input modalities simultaneously with graceful degradation when combined with environmental interference (IR lighting washing out eye-gaze cameras, electromagnetic interference from hospital equipment disrupting Bluetooth switches). Test each prototype against RESNA AT outcome measurement frameworks and predict vocabulary selection speed (words/minute) and user fatigue onset timing for each persona under simulated conversation scenarios requiring both pre-stored phrases and novel word construction.



Think carefully about how to solve this task. For each step:
1. State what you need to do
2. Explain WHY this step is necessary
3. Describe the expected output

- "internal_reasoning": brief approach explanation
